task_completion.description: Jurism application is opened and the library items are visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism main window is open, displaying a list of legal references and articles.

task_completion.description: The library items are sorted by date to identify the oldest precedents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Date' column header in Jurism is clicked/active, showing items ordered chronologically (oldest first, starting with 1803).

task_completion.description: The three oldest items are selected for export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Marbury v. Madison (1803), The Path of the Law (1897), and Brown v. Board of Education (1954) are simultaneously highlighted/selected in the Jurism item list.

task_completion.description: The export dialog is opened and configured for CSV format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export...' dialog box is visible in Jurism, and the 'Format' dropdown is set to 'CSV'.

task_completion.description: The exported file is saved with the correct name and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows the destination folder as '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename as 'oldest_precedents.csv', or a file manager/terminal verifies the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jurism application interface (menus, right-clicks, export dialogs) rather than terminal commands querying the SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not select newer, forbidden items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Items like Obergefell v. Hodges (2015), Tinker v. Des Moines (1969), and Miranda v. Arizona (1966) are NOT highlighted or selected during the export step.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the CSV contents.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of text editors (like nano, gedit, or VS Code) or terminal echo commands being used to manually write the case names and dates into a CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exporting exactly three specific items: 1. Marbury v. Madison (1803), 2. The Path of the Law (1897), and 3. Brown v. Board of Education (1954). The export format must be CSV, and the file must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/oldest_precedents.csv'. Forbidden items that must NOT be selected include Obergefell v. Hodges (2015), Tinker v. Des Moines (1969), and Miranda v. Arizona (1966).
